ref: If a wild animal killed one of your sheep or goats, I paid for it myself. In fact, you demanded the full price, whether the animal was killed during the day or at night.
text: M…î…î nanemgbole hyele ye hunle ye b…îb…î la, meanva ye funli meamb…õl…õ w…î, mefa bie mesie …î bo zo. Saa b…õwua bie ewiazo anz…õ…õ n…î…îzo a, …õdie bie …õfi me …õk…õ ne.
translated: If you don't know the path you are walking, and you don't know where you are going, you will use a child to show you the way. If the child gets lost and cannot find the way, it is not your fault.

ref: Both this pile of rocks and this large rock have been set up between us as a reminder. I must never go past them to attack you, and you must never come past them to attack me.
text: Awol…õ edukue ne le dasel…õ y…õ…õ bol…õ sika ne le dasel…õ, k…õ menr…õb…î menr…õd…õla edukue …õhye menr…õra …õ nwo zo, y…õ…õ …õdaw…î noko …õnr…õb…î …õnr…õd…õla edukue ne nee bol…õ sika ne …õnr…õra me nwo zo k…õ …õbay…õ me …õtane.
translated: The money that is in the bag and the money that is in the pocket, if I don't spend it or use it, the bag and its contents, and the money in the pocket, will not be spent or used, and it will remain with me.

ref: Once again, Abraham bowed down
text: Na Ebileham dwazole b…île …î ti aze w…î H…õtema anyunlu,
translated: And Ebileham built an altar there and called on the name of the Lord at H…õtema.

ref: King Melchizedek of Salem was a priest of God Most High. He brought out some bread and wine
text: Na Seel…õm Belemgbunli M…õlekez…õd…õke r…õl…õle ye kpanwo nee nwanye. M…õlekez…õd…õke le Anwuma Anwuma Nyamenle ne s…õl…õvol…õ.
translated: The story of Belemgbunli M…õlekez…õd…õke is very interesting and enjoyable. M…õlekez…õd…õke is the daughter of Anwuma Anwuma Nyamenle.

ref: Jacob replied, ‚ÄúI am Esau, your first-born, and I have done what you told me. Please sit up and eat the meat I have brought. Then you can give me your blessing.‚Äù
text: Gyek…îbo buale k…õ, ‚ÄúMedame …õ ra nrenya kpanyinli Yis…î …î. Mey…õ m…î…î …õhanle …õhilele me la; dwazo t…õnla aze di me nane ale…õ ye, na yileyila me.‚Äù
translated: "Gyek…îbo said, 'I am not greater than John. I am not worthy to untie his sandals; he will come after me, and I am not worthy to carry his sandals.'"

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: We know that God is love, and those who abide in love abide in God, and God abides in them. Love is perfected in us when we have no fear.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: We have come to ask for forgiveness for our wrongdoings; let us all come together and unite as one family.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: When I was young and my mother was still alive, she used to tell me not to do bad things. Keena's grandmother also said that if you do good, it will be rewarded, and if you do bad, God will punish you.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: When the sun was setting and the shadows were growing long, the children gathered around, and the old woman began her story, "Listen carefully, for this tale is not just for today but for generations to come. And when I am gone, remember the lessons I have shared, so they may guide you in the days ahead."

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: When the child's mother and siblings went to the farm and left Gyek…îbo alone at home, he took his cutlass and went to Gyek…îbo's farm, and Gyek…îbo's dog followed him to the farm and bit him.

ref: and when the boy was eight days old, Abraham circumcised him, just as God had commanded.
text: Ayezeke lile kenle m…îtw…õ la, Ebileham y…õle ye mrenyanu nzonl…õ, k…õm…î Nyamenle zele ye la.
translated: When Ayezeke was still a child, Ebileham took him to the river, and that is where God met him.

ref: So the king told his men to let Abram and Sarai take their possessions and leave.
text: Na Yigyibiti belemgbunli ne manle ye menli ne dole ye nee …î ye adenle zo, na …îvale m…î…î …înyianle la amuala …îh…île.
translated: And the Lord God formed man from the dust of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath of life, and man became a living being.

ref: Esau asked Jacob, ‚ÄúWhy did you send those herds I met along the road?‚Äù ‚ÄúMaster,‚Äù Jacob answered, ‚ÄúI sent them so you would be friendly to me.‚Äù
text: Yis…î bizale ye k…õ, ‚ÄúNa ekpunli …õhende…õ ne m…î m…î…î me nee b…õ yiale la abo a le duzu?‚Äù Gyek…îbo buale k…õ, ‚ÄúB…õle me menle …õde…õ, amaa meaz…î …õ nye.‚Äù
translated: "The woman asked, 'Why is it that when I am with my husband, he doesn't give me anything?' The man replied, 'I don't have anything, that's why I don't give you.'"

ref: The man answered, ‚ÄúI was naked, and when I heard you walking through the garden, I was frightened and hid!‚Äù
text: Nrenya ne buale k…õ, ‚ÄúMendele …õ gyak…õ abo w…î tola ne anu, na …õnee mesulo, …îboalek…õ mel…õ me nwo z…î, y…õ…õ menveale a.‚Äù
translated: The woman said, "I have seen a snake in the bush, and when I saw it, the snake chased me, but I ran away."

ref: They kept on wrestling until the man said, ‚ÄúLet go of me! It's almost daylight.‚Äù ‚ÄúYou can't go until you bless me,‚Äù Jacob replied.
text: Akee …îzele Gyek…îbo k…õ, ‚ÄúGyakyi me maa meng…î, na ale…õ …õl…õkye.‚Äù Gyek…îbo hanle k…õ, ‚ÄúMenr…õgyakyi w…î menr…õmaa …õnr…õh…î, kyes…õ …õyila me.‚Äù
translated: When Akee asked Gyek…îbo, "Gyakyi gave me a child, and that is why I am happy." Gyek…îbo replied, "I will not Gyakyi because my child is not here, but I am still happy."

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.
text: Yem…îti Nyamenle b…île sonla k…õ …îdaye ye …õsaso la; …îdaye Nyamenle mumua ne ye …õsaso a …înleanle …îb…île sonla a. ∆Üb…île b…õ nrenya nee raal…õ.
translated: We have heard that God created the world and everything in it; God created the world and everything in it from nothing. The world did not come from anything else.

ref: Get some good lumber and build a boat. Put rooms in it and cover it with tar inside and out.
text: Yem…îti fa g…îfa baka bob…î …õl…õnemgbole; ky…õ nuhua ekpumgbunli ekpumgbunli na fa bilewu ka nuhua nee nwol…õ amuala.
translated: We have come to show you the way of truth; do not be afraid, be strong and courageous, and do not let fear overcome you.

ref: I will give you and them the land in which you are now a foreigner. I will give the whole land of Canaan to your family forever, and I will be their God.
text: Na mebava az…õl…õ ne m…î…î …õl…õkp…îsakp…îsa zol…õ la meamaa w…î nee …õ bo zo amra. B…õbava Keena az…õl…õ ne k…îs…î…îti b…õay…õ b…õ …õde…õ, na mebay…õ b…õ Nyamenle.‚Äù
translated: When I was young and my mother was still alive, she used to tell me not to go to the farm. Keena's children are young and they are still learning, but I am now old and wise.

ref: You didn't have much before I came, but the Lord has blessed everything I have ever done for you. Now it's time for me to start looking out for my own family.
text: ∆Üluak…õ …õnee w…î ny…õmoa le ekyi bie y…õ…õ membale a, k…õkala b…õ nwo …õzo d…î…înwo kpal…õ, y…õ…õ medame me ti, ∆êd…õnk…õma …õyila w…î w…î debie biala anu. Na k…õkala menea a akee yedwu mek…õ m…î…î …îw…î k…õ medwenle me sua nu amra anwo a.‚Äù
translated: When the rain falls and the clouds gather, the wind will blow from all directions, and the trees will sway, the leaves will fall, and the earth will tremble. It is as if the world is coming to an end. And when the wind blows, it carries away everything in its path, leaving nothing behind.

ref: When the man saw that he could not win, he struck Jacob on the hip and threw it out of joint.
text: Na m…î…î nrenya ne nwunle k…õ yeangola yeangomo Gyek…îbo anwo zo la, …îvale …î sa …îhanle Gyek…îbo …õz…îl…õ, na Gyek…îbo adwongu huanenle w…î …õfutul…õ ne anu.
translated: When the child was born and named Gyek…îbo, he grew up and became a great warrior, and Gyek…îbo's strength was known throughout the land and beyond.
